acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] IPC mutation success responses include DurabilityReceipt in JSON mode.
- [ ] Durability timeouts return ErrorPayload with retryable=true and receipt populated.
- [ ] ErrorPayload schema matches REALTIME_ERRORS.md (code/message/retryable/details/receipt).
- [ ] API JSON types expose receipts without lossy view structs.
description:
**Problem**
Phase 4: update IPC response shapes for receipts and error payloads. Current `src/daemon/ipc.rs` does not return DurabilityReceipt, and ErrorPayload does not match REALTIME_ERRORS.md.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.14 (IPC protocol evolution), §16.1 (response fields and error payload), §16.3 (CLI flags)

**Files:** src/daemon/ipc.rs, src/api/mod.rs, src/cli/mod.rs, src/cli/commands/*, REALTIME_ERRORS.md

**Design**
- Add DurabilityReceipt to success responses and to timeout errors when waiting for replicated durability.
- Replace ErrorPayload shape to `{ code, message, retryable, retry_after_ms?, details?, receipt? }` and keep error codes aligned with REALTIME_ERRORS.md.
- Ensure API JSON types expose receipts without lossy view structs.
- Request field additions and CLI flag plumbing live in bd-3m5.51.
design:
**Design**
- Add optional `namespace`, `durability`, and `client_request_id` fields to mutation requests with defaults (namespace=core, durability=LocalFsync).
- Add DurabilityReceipt to success responses and to timeout errors when waiting for replicated durability.
- Replace ErrorPayload shape to `{ code, message, retryable, retry_after_ms?, details?, receipt? }` and keep error codes aligned with REALTIME_ERRORS.md.
- Plumb CLI flags `--namespace`, `--durability`, `--client-request-id` to IPC requests.
title: Phase 4: IPC requests + receipts (namespace, durability, client_request_id)
Detect force-push and surface explicit warning in sync status

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Lost push races do not erase legacy source metadata or warnings from the returned migrate outcome.
- [ ] Already-canonical repos without origin report a no-remote disposition when the caller did not pass `--no-push`.
- [ ] Regression tests cover retry metadata preservation and the no-origin/no-op outcome case.
description:
**Problem**
Adversarial review found two outcome-contract problems. First, after a retryable push race, the returned migrate outcome can be recomputed from the eventual remote winner instead of the legacy source we actually tried to migrate, which sanitizes `from_effective_version`, deps format, converted-deps, and warnings. Second, an already-canonical repo with no origin currently returns `push: skipped_no_push` and `commit_oid: null`, which makes the rollout docs indistinguishable from a converged fleet-success case even though there was no remote to cut over.

**Files**: crates/beads-git/src/sync.rs, crates/beads-rs/src/cli/backend.rs, crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/migration.rs, MIGRATION.md
design:
**Design**
Capture the migration source summary before the first rewrite attempt and preserve it across retry loops, even if a later refetch converges on another replica's winner. Returned outcome metadata should describe the source that triggered migration, not whichever canonical ref happened to win the race.

Also distinguish no-origin/no-op from user-requested no-push: when the caller asked for a normal migrate but no origin exists, return a no-remote disposition even for the already-canonical no-op path. Update tests and docs to match.
title: Migration follow-up: keep outcome metadata truthful across retries and no-remote no-op cases

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Integration tests are grouped by feature.
- [ ] `cargo test` passes with the same coverage.
- [ ] No change to fixture behavior.
description:
**Problem**
- Integration tests are phase-based (`tests/phase*`) which makes it hard to find feature coverage.
- New contributors must know implementation phases to find tests.

**Files:**
- tests/phase*.rs
- tests/fixtures/*
design:
- Rename/move phase tests into feature-based files (`tests/wal_*`, `tests/repl_*`, `tests/checkpoint_*`, `tests/admin_*`, etc).
- Keep test contents unchanged; only file names/organization change.
- Update any references in docs if needed.
title: Reorganize integration tests by feature
